title: First Tapping Script: Three Minutes, No Jargon
subtitle: A simple EFT sequence for someone who has never tapped before
excerpt: A three-minute EFT tapping sequence for a first-time user. No explanation of meridians, no theory about why it works. Eight points, one round, one reframe — enough to show whether tapping is something worth going further with.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education as a first-contact practice. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've heard about tapping and want to try it without reading a lot first — or when you want to show someone else what it looks like.

content.text: A first tapping sequence — three minutes, one topic, no prerequisites. The topic is general money unease, which is specific enough to work with and broad enough to apply to most people who land here.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first — it explains the eight points, where they are, and why the script is said out loud.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the side of your hand and say each statement once:
Even though money makes me uneasy and I'm not sure why, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though there's something around money I haven't sorted out, I'm willing to look at it.
Even though money and I have a complicated relationship, I accept myself exactly as I am right now.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: Money makes me uneasy.
Side of Eye: I don't feel settled about it.
Under Eye: I keep checking the account.
Under Nose: Something about money doesn't feel safe.
Chin: I want to feel easy about this and I don't.
Collarbone: It's been like this for a while.
Under Arm: The unease is there even when the numbers are fine.
Top of Head: I'm ready to start working on this.
Reframe
Eyebrow: Money can feel steadier than this.
Side of Eye: The unease is not permanent.
Under Eye: I give myself permission to feel calmer about this.
Under Nose: I am safe to look at this directly.
Chin: Money and I can have a different relationship.
Collarbone: I have started. That is something.
Under Arm: I am ready for the next step.
Top of Head: I am open to money feeling easy.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
